Name: WCET Analysis Initiation and Scoping
Description: Defining the specific functional module, hardware platform, and operational constraints for which the worst-case execution time must be determined.

Name: Characterize Code and Program Dependencies
Description: Mapping out all control flow paths, data dependencies, and potential external resource accesses (e.g., I/O, network calls) that affect execution time.

Name: Model the Execution Environment and Assumptions
Description: Establishing the system model, including details on cache behavior, memory hierarchy, operating system scheduling jitter, and interrupt handling latency.

Name: Define Boundary Condition Test Vectors
Description: Generating extreme input data sets (maximum array size, minimum/maximum valid inputs, rapid change sequences) designed to force the code down every potential critical path.

Name: Set Up Timing Measurement Tools
Description: Configuring hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulators and specialized timing analysis tools (e.g., specialized debuggers, emulators) to measure execution time accurately.

Name: Execute and Collect Timing Data
Description: Running the test vector set (from 2003) repeatedly under the controlled environment (from 2004) to record the maximum observed execution time for each critical path.

Name: Analyze Performance Metrics and Identify Jitter
Description: Processing the raw data to determine the true statistical worst-case execution time (WCET), identifying sources of timing variability (jitter), and pinpointing performance bottlenecks.

Name: Iterative Code Refinement and Model Update
Description: If the calculated WCET violates timing requirements, the source code must be refactored (e.g., to eliminate cache thrashing or excessive locks), and the execution model must be updated to reflect the change.

Name: Formal Verification and Final Validation
Description: Performing a final, comprehensive run of the refactored code through the entire test suite and formal model to certify that the newly calculated WCET meets all safety and performance requirements.

Name: Interference Analysis Planning and Scope Definition
Description: Defining the system boundaries, the set of interacting components (tasks), and the critical shared resources (CPU, memory bus, peripherals) that must be analyzed for contention.

Name: Hardware Platform Characterization (Interference Sources)
Description: Detailed analysis of the physical hardware constraints, including cache coherence protocols, memory bus timing limits, DMA capabilities, and peripheral arbitration mechanisms.

Name: Model Shared Resource Consumption
Description: Creating a detailed model of how each task accesses and utilizes shared resources (e.g., cache lines, memory bandwidth, mutexes) based on the hardware constraints identified.

Name: Identify Task Dependencies and Conflicts
Description: Performing pairwise interaction analysis to map all potential and actual overlaps between task executions and shared resource access (e.g., Task A waiting for Task B to release a critical section).

Name: Quantify Worst-Case Interference Impact
Description: Using analytical methods to mathematically determine the maximum detrimental effect one task can have on another's timing or data integrity due to the identified conflicts.

Name: Assess Safety and Timing Violations
Description: Comparing the calculated worst-case interference limits against the established safety margins and hard real-time deadlines. Determining if the system is non-schedulable or unsafe due to contention.

Name: Develop Mitigation and Isolation Strategies
Description: Designing countermeasures to eliminate or reduce observed interference. This may involve resource partitioning (time/space), priority modification, or introducing synchronization barriers.

Name: Re-Analyze and Validate Mitigation Effectiveness
Description: Rerunning the interference quantification process using the modified system model and resource allocation to prove that the newly implemented strategy eliminates all critical violations.

Name: Document Final Safety Case
Description: Compiling the complete documentation, including all assumptions, test results, and formal proof of resource isolation, to satisfy regulatory safety sign-off.

Name: Hardware Interference Analysis Planning and Scope Definition
Description: Establishing the scope of the hardware analysis: identifying the physical boundaries, timing metrics, and components where contention is possible (CPU, Memory, I/O, Bus).

Name: Microbenchmarking Environment Setup
Description: Configuring the test harness, profiling tools, and isolated test modules to ensure that measured performance metrics are as close as possible to the bare hardware performance, minimizing overhead.

Name: Cache and Memory Hierarchy Analysis (Benchmarked)
Description: Using controlled microbenchmarks to quantify cache hit/miss ratios, measure cache line transfer rates, and detect cross-core/cross-process cache interference.

Name: System Bus and Memory Bandwidth Analysis (Benchmarked)
Description: Executing microbenchmarks designed to saturate the system bus and memory controller to quantify total usable bandwidth, transaction overhead, and potential queuing delays under load.

Name: I/O and Peripheral Interaction Analysis
Description: Analyzing dedicated I/O resources, DMA capabilities, and interrupt handling overhead using controlled benchmark tasks to quantify timing impact.

Name: Synthesize Resource Interaction Model
Description: Combining the quantitative findings (cache, bus, I/O) into a single, predictive model that predicts total, simultaneous, multi-source interference effects, factoring in measured overheads.

Name: Analyze Timing Jitter and Non-Determinism
Description: Focusing on unpredictability (e.g., OS jitter, context switching variability) by running statistical benchmarking over long periods to determine the upper bound of variability.

Name: Generate Hardware Stress Test Vectors
Description: Creating workload mixes and input sequences designed to intentionally force the hardware into its absolute worst-case combination of resource contention, validated by prior microbenchmarking results.

Name: Execute and Measure Interference Stress Tests
Description: Running the generated stress vectors on the hardware to measure the true, worst-case degradation in performance metrics and validating the predictions made by the microbenchmarks.

Name: Final Analysis and Safety Case Generation
Description: Synthesizing all hardware findings, proposing concrete hardware or software mitigation strategies, and generating the final certified report proving the system's deterministic behavior.
